bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just begun (t=0.0). As a first move, I am proposing my most preferred outcome to anchor the negotiation and signal my priorities. I've selected the highest utility value for each issue.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02). The opponent's first offer provides me with very low utility (around 0.27), indicating conflicting preferences. To signal a cooperative spirit while protecting my top priorities (issue C and D), I will make a small concession on issue A and issue D, keeping my overall utility above 0.87.

Since my most important issues are issueC and issueD, I will maintain high utility on these by requesting my top value for issueC and issueD. To demonstrate a willingness to negotiate, I will make a slight concession on issueA and issueB, which have the lowest importance weights for me, proposing values that still yield decent utility.

I am making another high-utility offer by proposing my second-best value for my most important issue (issueC), while retaining top values for other important issues to probe for acceptable compromises.

Because my most heavily weighted issues are issueC and issueD, I will hold firm on high-utility values for these (valueC and valueD) while continuing to explore alternative combinations for my lower-weighted issues (issueA and issueB), aiming to uncover a compromise that satisfies the opponent without significantly impacting my overall score.

This targeted concession maintains a high utility for me (0.82) while potentially breaking their stalemate.
